title: calceolaria asperula
intro: Calceolaria asperula, also known as sticky mint or sticky slipper, is a flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and reducing fever. Calceolaria asperula can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
